title: GS1 Digital Link
objectives: Explain the Web URL syntax for GS1 identifiers
Resolve a scan to the right content
Encode a GS1 Digital Link in QR / DataMatrix

sections.markdown: **GS1 Digital Link** is a web-friendly URL syntax for GS1 identifiers. Instead of a raw GTIN, a product carries a URL like `https://id.gs1.org/01/08901234000015`. Scanning it in any camera opens a web page — no special app needed — and the URL can carry batch/serial/expiry info too.

sections.code:
Structure: https://<resolver>/<Primary key>/<value>[/<qualifier>/<value>]*[?params]

Examples:
  https://id.gs1.org/01/08901234000015
  https://id.gs1.org/01/08901234000015/10/BATCH42/17/261231/21/SN-777
    01 = GTIN
    10 = Batch/Lot     (Application Identifier)
    17 = Expiry (YYMMDD)
    21 = Serial number

The resolver returns different content per context:
  Consumer   -> product page / recipes
  Regulator  -> compliance data
  Pharmacist -> track-and-trace data

sections.markdown: Digital Link solves the '**one barcode, many uses**' problem. A single 2D code (QR/DataMatrix) can serve consumers (marketing/nutrition), retailers (POS scan → GTIN), regulators (traceability), and product authentication — because a resolver dispatches by requesting context / Accept headers.
Assuming a QR from an unknown source is a GS1 Digital Link. Validate the structure: valid resolver + `/01/` + a real GTIN + valid AIs. Treat unrecognized URLs as untrusted content, not identifiers.

For QR + Digital Link, place a GS1-approved resolver URL like `id.gs1.org` (or your own brand resolver). Include the minimum AIs on-pack; carry extended data (photos, allergens) in the resolver response — smaller printed code, easier to change online.

flashcards.back: A web URL syntax for GS1 identifiers that anyone with a camera can 'scan' — no special app.

flashcards.front: How to encode batch and expiry?
flashcards.back: Use Application Identifiers in the URL path: /10/<lot>/17/<yymmdd>/21/<serial>.

flashcards.front: How does one code serve many audiences?
flashcards.back: The resolver returns different content by context/Accept — consumer vs regulator vs POS.

options: A Web URL syntax for GS1 identifiers
quiz.explain: URL-based — works with any smartphone camera.

quiz.q: One code, many uses works via…
options: A resolver that dispatches by context

challenges.text: Parse a Digital Link URL and extract GTIN + batch.
Emit a valid Digital Link with GTIN + expiry + serial.
Build a resolver stub that returns different JSON for consumer vs regulator Accept headers.
Migrate an existing SKU line from EAN-13 to a single Digital Link QR on-pack.
